source.title: Enterprise mass email architecture and third-party analysis integration
source.summary: Enterprise email is usually a layered stack. The best TextTraits role is an analytical scoring layer that attaches at authoring, pre-send, and post-send points instead of becoming a sending platform.

recommendation.positioning: TextTraits should be sold and tested as a pre-send and post-send intelligence layer for existing marketing, sales engagement, CRM, CDP, and delivery stacks.
why: Journey and workflow layers see intent, audience, trigger reason, and message context before delivery.
Pre-send gates can prevent defective campaigns without owning subscriber state or IP reputation.
Post-send webhooks and warehouse exports let the model learn from delivery, bounce, complaint, open, click, and suppression outcomes.
avoid: Owning suppression, consent, subscriber state, or delivery IP reputation as the default product posture.
SMTP content rewriting after DKIM signing unless the deployment is explicitly designed for it.

layers.name: Authoring lint
layers.where: Campaign builder, template editor, browser extension, or template CI/CD path.
layers.best_for: Immediate marketer feedback on copy, links, tokens, disclaimers, tone, and missing evidence.
layers.failure_mode: Fail open with warnings unless a policy rule is explicitly blocking.
layers.prototype_surface: Inline checklist beside the email editor.

layers.name: Pre-send gate
layers.where: Publish, schedule, journey action, workflow step, or API send middleware.
layers.best_for: Blocking or warning on compliance, deliverability, personalization, brand, and clarity issues before launch.
layers.latency_target: Hard timeout, with p95 under a few hundred milliseconds when directly on a send path.
layers.failure_mode: Configurable fail-open or fail-closed by rule family.
layers.prototype_surface: Pass, warn, or block decision with evidence and remediation.

layers.name: Post-send analytics
layers.where: Provider webhooks, event streams, warehouse backfills, or BI/CDP exports.
layers.best_for: Outcome monitoring, complaint and bounce triage, trend analysis, benchmarking, and model tuning.
layers.latency_target: Near-real-time for webhooks; batch throughput and reproducibility for historical analysis.
layers.failure_mode: Retry, deduplicate, and never block already-accepted delivery events.
layers.prototype_surface: Outcome scorecards joined back to campaign, template, and send IDs.

targets.label: HubSpot workflow action
targets.best_use_case: Marketing or sales teams that already coordinate outreach through HubSpot workflows and need a review gate before an email step.
targets.integration_layer: Custom workflow action plus CRM/app-card review surface.
targets.experiment: Simulate a workflow action that posts subject, body, contact segment, subscription state, and campaign IDs to TextTraits, then returns pass, warn, or block plus findings.
targets.risk: Moderate. The app must keep consent and subscription state authoritative in HubSpot.

targets.label: Salesforce Journey Builder gate
targets.best_use_case: Large campaign teams with Content Builder assets, Journey Builder flows, and formal approval before publish or schedule.
targets.integration_layer: Journey Builder custom activity plus Content Builder asset analysis.
targets.experiment: Mock a Journey Builder activity that analyzes template metadata, sample render context, campaign identifiers, headers, and links before allowing a journey to continue.
targets.risk: Higher setup cost, but strong fit for enterprise governance.

targets.label: Braze Canvas and Currents loop
targets.best_use_case: Lifecycle teams that need real-time journey scoring and a feedback loop from engagement streams.
targets.integration_layer: Canvas pre-send scoring plus Currents event ingestion.
targets.experiment: Score a Canvas email step, then join simulated delivery and engagement events back to the original analysis request.
targets.risk: Requires careful token and live-content handling because final render can differ from saved templates.

targets.label: SendGrid or SES API middleware
targets.best_use_case: Product or transactional email systems that already send through SendGrid, Amazon SES, SparkPost, or Mailchimp Transactional.
targets.integration_layer: API middleware before provider send endpoint plus event webhook ingestion.
targets.experiment: Analyze the final payload before provider submission and attach a deterministic analysis_request_id for webhook joins.
targets.risk: Latency-sensitive. Needs strict timeout, idempotency, and rate-limit handling.

targets.label: CDP and warehouse feedback
targets.best_use_case: Teams that want benchmark dashboards, governance reporting, suppression intelligence, and model retraining data across vendors.
targets.integration_layer: Batch worker reading campaign, template, send, event, and suppression partitions.
targets.experiment: Load historical campaign rows, reconstruct cohorts, compute TextTraits findings, and publish a normalized findings table.
targets.risk: Not suitable for blocking sends, but strongest for learning and cross-platform visibility.
